answer:
verdict: prob no statutory damages for this pirate — u missed the window

rule (17 USC §412): statutory damages + atty fees only if work was registered b4 the infringement began, OR w/in 3 mo of 1st publication. u pubbed mid-jan 2026, so grace period closed ~mid-april. its july 5 now, so registering today wont cover infringement that already started

key wrinkle: even if piracy continues after u register, courts treat ongoing infringement as 1 continuing act that "commenced" pre-registration → still barred. only a genuinely new infringement by a diff party after registration wld qualify

what u can still do:
1. register NOW anyway — required b4 u can even file suit (fourth estate v wall-street.com), + covers u vs future infringers. ~$45-65 online, or ~$800 special handling if u need it fast for litigation
2. sue for actual damages + infringer's profits (no registration timing bar on those) — tho vs anon pirate site thats often hard to collect
3. DMCA takedown notice to the site + its host + google — no registration needed, free, usually the fastest practical fix
4. hit their payment processors/ads if they monetize

realistically: takedown notice is ur best move, register today for go-forward protection
